sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Devon Energy (NYSE:DVN) Given New $67.00 Price Target at Piper Sandler
feed.summary: Piper Sandler has increased its price target for Devon Energy (NYSE:DVN) from $59.00 to $67.00, maintaining an "overweight" rating and suggesting a potential upside of 45.67%. This new target is significantly higher than the consensus analyst target of $48.85, although the stock currently holds a "Moderate Buy" rating from analysts. Devon Energy recently reported strong quarterly earnings of $0.82 per share, surpassing analyst estimates, despite a slight year-over-year revenue decrease.

feed.title: Piper Sandler Increases ConocoPhillips (NYSE:COP) Price Target to $154.00
feed.summary: Piper Sandler has raised its price target for ConocoPhillips (NYSE:COP) to $154.00 from $111.00, maintaining an "overweight" rating. This new target suggests a potential upside of 27.93% from the current share price. The move comes despite ConocoPhillips missing last quarter's earnings and revenue estimates, and significant insider selling over the past 90 days.

feed.title: Celanese (NYSE:CE) Price Target Raised to $81.00 at Citigroup
feed.summary: Citigroup has increased its price target for Celanese (NYSE:CE) from $75.00 to $81.00, maintaining a "buy" rating and suggesting a potential upside of 34.71%. This comes despite Celanese missing its recent quarterly earnings and revenue estimates. While analyst coverage remains mixed, other firms have also adjusted their price targets and ratings, with some recent upgrades along with earlier downgrades in response to earnings and market conditions.

feed.title: Citigroup Forecasts Strong Price Appreciation for Westlake (NYSE:WLK) Stock
feed.summary: Citigroup has raised its price target for Westlake (NYSE:WLK) to $130, maintaining a "buy" rating and projecting a 14.6% upside. Despite this optimistic outlook, overall analyst sentiment for Westlake is mixed, with a consensus "Hold" rating and an average target price of $98.13. The company recently reported an EPS beat but a revenue decline year-over-year, alongside negative net margins, negative P/E, and recent insider share sales by its chairman and CFO.

feed.title: Here’s Why MSCI Could Deliver 78% Upside
feed.summary: MSCI, a global index and financial data provider, shows potential for 78% upside according to TIKR's valuation model. This is driven by strong Q4 2025 earnings, record ETF inflows, a BlackRock license extension, and strategic acquisitions and AI advancements. While facing political headwinds in its Sustainability and Climate segment in the Americas, the company's compounding structural advantages and projected margin expansion suggest significant growth potential.

feed.title: EOG Resources (NYSE:EOG) Price Target Raised to $144.00 at Piper Sandler
feed.summary: Piper Sandler has increased its price target for EOG Resources (NYSE:EOG) to $144.00 from $127.00, maintaining a "neutral" rating and suggesting a 6.3% upside. This follows EOG's strong quarterly performance, where it exceeded analyst expectations with $2.27 EPS and $5.64 billion in revenue. Despite mixed analyst sentiment, with some firms raising targets and others downgrading, the stock traded near its 52-week high, and institutional investors hold a significant portion of its shares.

feed.title: Norwegian Cruise Line to launch new ship at PortMiami
feed.summary: Norwegian Cruise Line's newest Luna ship is scheduled to begin Caribbean sailings from PortMiami this month. This launch highlights the cruise line's continued investment in the Miami port and the broader travel and tourism sector. The article also mentions related news regarding other cruise lines and their operations.

feed.title: SLB Middle East Disruptions Test Revenue Outlook And Valuation Upside
feed.summary: SLB (NYSE:SLB) is experiencing operational disruptions in parts of the Middle East due to escalating regional events, leading the company to expect lower first-quarter revenue and higher costs. As a major global oilfield services provider, these disruptions highlight the region's importance to the sector and the potential for shifts in operational conditions. Investors should monitor management updates regarding contract commitments, customer activity, and broader operational adjustments, as well as the duration and impact on future quarters.

feed.title: Assessing American Express (AXP) Valuation After Recent Share Price Weakness
feed.summary: American Express (AXP) has seen recent share price weakness, with declines over the past 30 and 90 days, despite strong longer-term returns. At a share price of US$305.99, one narrative suggests it is 70% undervalued with a fair value of $308.19 due to consistent revenue growth, expanding card fees, and firm profit margins. However, potential challenges include slowed growth in new cards or regulatory/geopolitical issues.

feed.title: Mosaic Turns Fertilizer Byproduct Into Rare Earths Opportunity In Brazil
feed.summary: Mosaic (NYSE:MOS) has partnered with Rainbow Rare Earths on the Uberaba project in Brazil to extract rare earths like neodymium and praseodymium from phosphogypsum, a fertilizer byproduct. This initiative diversifies Mosaic's business beyond fertilizers into clean energy materials and positions it alongside companies like Rio Tinto and MP Materials. While offering potential new revenue streams and waste utilization, the project introduces technical, financial, and environmental risks that investors should consider alongside Mosaic's existing fertilizer operations and expansion plans.

feed.title: Adobe sued for trademark infringement over 'Foundry' AI tool
feed.summary: The Foundry Visionmongers, a British visual-effects software company, has sued Adobe for trademark infringement over its "Firefly Foundry" suite of AI tools. Foundry claims Adobe's use of the "Foundry" name for similar content-creation software will cause consumer confusion, especially since Foundry's own software also has AI capabilities and has been used in Oscar-winning visual effects. The lawsuit seeks a court order to prevent Adobe from using the name and unspecified monetary damages.

feed.title: Is GE HealthCare (GEHC) Quietly Redefining Its Moat With FDA-Cleared Cloud Imaging and AI?
feed.summary: GE HealthCare recently secured FDA 510(k) clearance for its Genesis View diagnostic viewer and showcased a suite of AI-powered, cloud-first software solutions at HIMSS 2026, aiming to enhance imaging and clinical workflow efficiency. While these developments bolster product competitiveness and support liquidity for AI investments, the company's investment narrative still faces risks from tariffs and trade policies, which could impact margins and cash flow. Despite these challenges, GE HealthCare projects significant revenue and earnings growth by 2028, leading to a fair value estimate that suggests a substantial upside.

feed.title: AstraZeneca’s DURGA-4 CAR-T Study Puts New Pressure on Multiple Myeloma Rivals
feed.summary: AstraZeneca (GB:AZN) has announced an update on its DURGA-4 Phase 3 clinical study for AZD0120, a dual-target CAR-T cell therapy for relapsed refractory multiple myeloma. The study, which commenced in January 2026 and is actively recruiting, compares AZD0120 against standard drug regimens. Positive results could bolster AstraZeneca's oncology portfolio, intensify competition for rivals like Johnson & Johnson and Bristol Myers Squibb, and validate dual-target CAR-T platforms.

feed.title: How Investors May Respond To EQT (EQT) Aggressively Retiring US$1.15 Billion Of Senior Notes
feed.summary: EQT Corporation is aggressively retiring US$1.15 billion of senior notes through a cash tender offer and redemption of its 2027 notes. This move is aimed at reducing debt and reshaping its maturity profile, consistent with EQT's ongoing deleveraging strategy and focus on shareholder returns. Investors are weighing EQT's sustained natural gas demand from AI and power generation against long-term decarbonization risks.

feed.title: CEO Albert Bourla Reflects on Pfizer’s Year of Bold Moves and Relentless Innovation in 2025 Annual Review
feed.summary: Pfizer's CEO, Albert Bourla, reviewed 2025 as a year of "bold moves and relentless innovation," marked by strong performance and global impact for patients. The company focused on advancing R&D in oncology, vaccines, and immunology, while also prioritizing strategic partnerships to deliver medicines worldwide. Pfizer emphasized strengthening scientific leadership through expanded R&D capabilities and the integration of artificial intelligence to accelerate drug discovery and development.

feed.title: Tractor Supply Co. Announces National Finals Rodeo Sponsorship
feed.summary: Tractor Supply Co. has significantly deepened its commitment to the rodeo world by becoming the presenting sponsor of the National Finals Rodeo (NFR). This builds upon their existing involvement, including the "Red Rider Program" for employee competitors and collaborations with major rodeo organizations like the PRCA. The 2026 NFR will now be titled "The Wrangler National Finals Rodeo presented by Tractor Supply Company," highlighting their prominent new role.

feed.title: Why Raymond James left more confident in WEX’s (WEX) 2026 growth path
feed.summary: This article from MSN focuses on Raymond James's increased confidence in WEX's growth trajectory through 2026. The financial firm likely analyzed WEX's performance, strategic initiatives, and market position to reach this positive outlook. The report suggests WEX is well-positioned for sustained growth in the coming years.

feed.title: Viatris-Backed Nyxol Trial Completes Phase 3, Setting Up a Key Night-Vision Catalyst
feed.summary: Viatris Inc. (VTRS) and Opus Genetics, Inc. (IRD) announced the completion of a Phase 3 clinical trial for Nyxol (0.75% phentolamine ophthalmic solution), an eye drop designed to improve low-light and night vision for patients who have visual disturbances after refractive surgery. The trial's completion reduces development risk, but market reaction for involved companies will likely remain muted until the efficacy and safety data are publicly released. Positive results could create a new revenue stream for Viatris in specialty eye care and significantly impact Opus Genetics' valuation.

feed.title: Ross to Open New Stores in the United States: When and Where
feed.summary: Ross Stores, Inc. (ROST) has announced the opening of 17 new Ross Dress for Less and dd’s DISCOUNTS stores across 11 states in the US between February and March. The company plans to open a total of approximately 110 new stores in fiscal year 2026. Ross offers high-quality, branded clothing and home items at significant discounts, sourcing excess inventory and liquidations to provide affordable prices to its target audience of 18-54 year olds.

feed.title: Is American States Water’s (AWR) Bigger 2026 Capex Plan Quietly Redefining Its Core Earnings Story?
feed.summary: American States Water (AWR) reported improved Q4 2025 diluted EPS of US$0.74 and announced a substantial 2026 capital expenditure plan of US$185 million to US$225 million. This increased investment reinforces the company's commitment to infrastructure and operational resilience, driving its regulated rate base and potential earnings. However, investors should be aware of potential earnings volatility due to regulatory factors like revenue decoupling and limited cost recovery.

feed.title: Assessing Applied Materials (AMAT) Valuation After New AI Memory Partnerships With Micron And SK Hynix
feed.summary: Applied Materials (AMAT) has recently secured long-term collaborations with Micron and SK Hynix for next-generation AI memory, driving significant share price appreciation. Despite strong momentum and analyst targets above its current price, a "narrative fair value" suggests it might be overvalued at US$351, though its P/E ratio is still below sector and peer averages. The article discusses key catalysts like growing semiconductor equipment demand from AI and government incentives, alongside risks from export controls and cyclical chip spending.

feed.title: Blackstone Stock Sits 45% Below Its Highs: Here’s What Analysts See Next
feed.summary: Blackstone (BX) stock is trading near its 52-week low, 45% below its peak, primarily due to redemption pressure on its BCRED private credit fund. Despite these redemption challenges, analysts remain broadly constructive on BX, citing its robust fee-earning engine, substantial dry powder, and significant projected free cash flow growth. The TIKR model suggests a 67% total return by December 2030, while Wall Street analysts have a mean price target implying 51.2% upside.

feed.title: Fifth Third Bank to go from zero to dozens of Houston-area branches with Comerica deal, new openings
feed.summary: Fifth Third Bank plans to open 50 to 60 new branches in the Houston area, marking its entry into the market. This expansion is part of a larger initiative to open 150 branches across Texas over three years, following a deal that includes acquiring Comerica's local branches. Currently, Fifth Third Bank has no presence in Houston.

feed.title: Archer-Daniels-Midland Should See Higher Near-Term Profits From Middle East War-Related Volatility
feed.summary: Morningstar analyst Seth Goldstein predicts that Archer-Daniels-Midland (ADM) will experience higher near-term profits due to volatility stemming from the Middle East war. The article, published on March 12, 2026, reiterates a previous assessment that ADM's profits could increase in 2026 if the conflict persists. This outlook follows a recent drop in ADM shares after its earnings guidance fell below consensus estimates.

feed.title: Why BWS Financial Sees A10 Networks (ATEN) Benefiting From Rising AI Network Traffic
feed.summary: BWS Financial raised its price target on A10 Networks (ATEN) to $28 from $24 and maintained a Buy rating, citing the company's strong position to benefit from increasing AI-related network traffic. A10 Networks reported record fourth-quarter and full-year 2025 revenues, driven by demand for AI infrastructure and high-throughput networking. The company projects 10% to 12% revenue growth for 2026 and boasts a 79% gross margin, with an even stronger growth outlook through 2028.

feed.title: How Akamai’s (AKAM) Blackwell GPU Buildout Expands Its Distributed AI Platform Ambitions
feed.summary: Akamai Technologies (AKAM) announced its plan to deploy thousands of NVIDIA Blackwell GPUs to expand its global distributed cloud infrastructure. This initiative aims to create a widely distributed AI platform accessible across its network for AI research, fine-tuning, optimization, and inference workloads. The platform is designed to reduce latency and data egress challenges by routing inference workloads to optimized compute resources in its extensive network of over 4,400 locations worldwide.

feed.title: Northern Trust Enters Fast-Growing ETF Servicing Race With Intercontinental Exchange Partnership
feed.summary: Northern Trust (NASDAQ: NTRS) is launching a U.S. ETF servicing platform in partnership with Intercontinental Exchange (NYSE: ICE) to meet growing institutional demand for ETFs. This collaboration leverages ICE's ETF Hub for order-taking, integrating it with Northern Trust's automated infrastructure for fund administration, custody, and transfer agency services. The partnership aims to expand Northern Trust's ETF servicing capabilities in the U.S. and highlights the increasing role of technology and automation in the ETF industry.

feed.title: Caterpillar demonstrates Cat AI Assistant at ConExpo-Con/Agg
feed.summary: Caterpillar showcased its new Cat AI Assistant at ConExpo-Con/Agg, a tool designed to help equipment owners, operators, and technicians quickly access machine data and troubleshooting guides using natural language. The AI assistant leverages Caterpillar's data sources to provide accurate insights for identifying issues, optimizing operations, and assisting with repairs, without summarization or hallucination. It aims to integrate seamlessly into daily workflows, acting as a coach for operators and a rapid information source for technicians.

feed.title: DMA designated Gatekeepers
feed.summary: The European Commission has designated several major technology companies, including Alphabet, Amazon, Apple, ByteDance, Meta, and Microsoft, as "gatekeepers" under the Digital Markets Act (DMA). This designation, which began in September 2023 and has seen updates through May 2024, subjects 23 core platform services to specific regulations. Notably, Booking.com was designated a gatekeeper in May 2024, while Facebook Marketplace was undesignated in April 2025.

feed.title: Parking lot solar-GPU canopy deployment planned in Texas
feed.summary: Auddia Inc., an AI music platform company merging with LT350, announced a non-binding Letter of Intent with an unnamed NYSE-listed medical REIT to pilot solar-integrated AI micro-data center canopies in a Dallas-Fort Worth hospital parking lot. LT350's technology integrates modular GPU, memory, and battery storage into solar canopies, enabling high-performance AI deployment in existing parking spaces. This initiative aims to provide secure, on-premise AI inference capabilities directly adjacent to healthcare operations, with plans for broader expansion if the pilot is successful.

feed.title: Short Interest in FS KKR Capital Corp. (NYSE:FSK) Expands By 104.9%
feed.summary: Short interest in FS KKR Capital Corp. (NYSE:FSK) surged by 104.9% in February to over 9.3 million shares, indicating increased bearish sentiment. This comes as the company missed Q4 earnings and revenue estimates, leading to analyst downgrades and a special dividend announcement. Insiders have recently purchased shares, while institutional investors hold a significant portion of the company's stock.

feed.title: McDonald's Bets Big On Value With $3 Menu: Will Consumers & Investors Win?
feed.summary: McDonald's is launching a new "McValue 2.0" menu with items priced at $3 or less and $4 breakfast meals to reinforce its leadership in value offerings. This initiative follows the success of previous value deals and aims to attract more consumers, particularly those with lower incomes, who are facing increased cost-of-living pressures. Analysts are positive about the strategy, which has contributed to strong financial results and a positive outlook for the company's stock, currently trading near all-time highs.

feed.title: Goldman Sachs Trims Derivative-Based Stake in Siemens Energy to Just Under 5%
feed.summary: Goldman Sachs has reduced its derivative-based voting stake in Siemens Energy to 4.98%, down from 5.38%. The position is primarily held through various financial instruments rather than direct shares, indicating a minor repositioning by the financial institution. Siemens Energy AG is a Germany-based energy technology group focused on power generation and transmission.

feed.title: Snap On Incorporated : on TSS to Showcase Heavy-Duty Solutions at TMC
feed.summary: Snap-on Total Shop Solutions (TSS) will exhibit its heavy-duty equipment and diagnostic solutions at the 2026 Technology and Maintenance Council (TMC) Annual Meeting in Nashville, Tennessee. Brands like Challenger Lifts, Hofmann, JOSAM, Mitchell 1, NEXIQ, and Pro-Cut will demonstrate their latest products, aiming to boost productivity for heavy-duty service professionals. The event will showcase innovations in tire changing, balancing, lifts, aligners, and comprehensive diagnostic tools.

feed.title: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman LLC Urges Enphase Energy,
feed.summary: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman LLC has filed a class action lawsuit against Enphase Energy, Inc. (NASDAQ: ENPH), alleging that the company and its officers made misleading statements regarding its ability to manage channel inventory and mitigate the effects of the 25D Credit termination. The lawsuit, on behalf of investors who purchased securities between April 22, 2025, and October 28, 2025, seeks to recover damages for federal securities law violations. The firm encourages affected investors to join the case and notes that the deadline to request appointment as lead plaintiff is April 20, 2026.

feed.title: FedEx introduces reusable shipping boxes for B2B shipments
feed.summary: FedEx has launched a new system of reusable shipping boxes for B2B shipments, developed in collaboration with Returnity. These collapsible boxes are designed for "closed-loop" environments like warehouse-to-store transfers, aiming to reduce packaging waste and improve efficiency. The initiative is part of FedEx's goal to achieve carbon-neutral global operations by 2040 and is expected to cut packaging costs and carbon emissions.

feed.title: Hyperscale Data Announces Estimated Total Assets and Net Assets per Share
feed.summary: Hyperscale Data, an AI data center company, announced its estimated total assets were $314 million ($0.87 per share) and net assets were $119 million ($0.33 per share) as of February 28, 2026. The company emphasizes its strong balance sheet, significant Bitcoin holdings, and commitment to expanding its digital asset portfolio and high-performance computing infrastructure. Executive Chairman Milton "Todd" Ault III reiterated confidence in Bitcoin's long-term value despite market volatility.

feed.title: Hershey: The secret to success and what’s next
feed.summary: The article explores The Hershey Company's enduring success, attributing it to founder Milton Hershey's early focus on mass production and strong brand-building. Despite a recent 60% profit decline and declining preference among younger generations in the US, core brands like Hershey's and Reese's remain dominant. The piece emphasizes the importance of global expansion and diversification into salty snacks and bold flavors to attract Gen Z and Gen A consumers, suggesting these are critical for Hershey's future growth and continued position among the top confectionery companies.

feed.title: Algoma Steel Group (NASDAQ:ASTL) Releases Earnings Results, Misses Estimates By $2.43 EPS
feed.summary: Algoma Steel Group (NASDAQ:ASTL) reported its quarterly earnings, missing analyst estimates significantly with a loss of ($2.11) EPS against a consensus of $0.32, and revenue of $331.3 million versus an expected $474.3 million. The company widened its Q4 loss to C$364.7 million and saw its cash reserves fall, raising liquidity concerns. Despite completing a strategic transition to an electric arc furnace (EAF) aiming for long-term operational and environmental benefits, one-time transition costs negatively impacted the results, leading to a stock price drop and analyst downgrades.

feed.title: Astera Labs to Participate in the 38th Annual Roth Conference
feed.summary: Astera Labs announced its participation in the 38th Annual Roth Conference in Dana Point, CA, on March 23, 2026. The company, a leader in AI infrastructure connectivity solutions, will present at 11:30 am PT, with a webcast available on its investor relations website. Astera Labs focuses on providing rack-scale AI infrastructure through its Intelligent Connectivity Platform and custom solutions.

feed.title: Wedbush Forecasts Strong Price Appreciation for AnaptysBio (NASDAQ:ANAB) Stock
feed.summary: Wedbush has increased its price target for AnaptysBio (NASDAQ:ANAB) stock to $75 from $60, maintaining an "outperform" rating, indicating a potential 21% upside. Despite a mixed market reaction to its latest earnings beat, the company's board authorized a $100 million stock buyback program, while insider sales were noted. AnaptysBio, a clinical-stage biotechnology company, focuses on therapeutic antibody development in immunology and inflammation.

feed.title: LRCX Stock Soars 33% in Three Months: Is There More Room for Growth?
feed.summary: Lam Research Corporation (LRCX) has seen its stock soar by 33% over the past three months, outperforming its industry and major competitors, driven by strong financial results and demand related to AI. The company's specialized technology in etch and deposition tools is critical for next-generation semiconductors, supporting its premium valuation. With robust growth forecasts and a Zacks Rank #1 (Strong Buy), Lam Research is considered a compelling investment despite high valuations.

feed.title: KLA Corporation Announces $7 Billion Share Buyback to Strengthen Investor Returns
feed.summary: KLA Corporation has launched a new $7 billion share buyback program, adding to an existing authorization, bringing the total repurchase capacity to $10.94 billion. This strategic move, along with a 21% increase in quarterly dividends, aims to strengthen investor returns and signal confidence in the company's position within the growing AI-driven semiconductor market. Despite market cyclicality and geopolitical tensions, KLA maintains its outlook and expects sustained leadership, with analysts projecting 20% annual returns.

feed.title: Gartner Announces Top Predictions for Data and Analytics in 2026
feed.summary: Gartner has released its top predictions for data and analytics (D&A) for 2026 and beyond, highlighting AI's pervasive impact across leadership, governance, talent, and market dynamics. The report emphasizes the blurring lines between human, machine, and organizational intelligence, with AI systems increasingly acting as collaborative partners. These predictions aim to provide leaders with a roadmap to navigate the evolving landscape and leverage opportunities presented by AI advancements.

feed.title: Corning Expands AI Data Center Portfolio: Will it Drive Growth?
feed.summary: Corning is expanding its AI data center portfolio by licensing PRIZM TMT optical ferrule technology to address the increasing demand for high-density connectivity in next-generation AI networks. This move positions Corning to capitalize on the growing AI infrastructure market, facing competition from companies like Amphenol and Ciena. Despite a strong stock rally, Corning's valuation is lower than the industry average, with positive revisions in its 2026 and 2027 earnings estimates.

feed.title: SHAREHOLDER ALERT: Lowey Dannenberg is Investigating Third Fifth Bancorp (NASDAQ: FITB) for Potential Breaches of Fiduciary Duty by Its Board of Directors
feed.summary: Lowey Dannenberg, P.C. has launched an investigation into Fifth Third Bancorp (NASDAQ: FITB) to determine if its officers and directors breached their fiduciary duties, potentially causing damages to the company and its shareholders. This investigation stems from a lawsuit filed on February 26, 2026, alleging that Fifth Third Bancorp ignored red flags and concealed a subprime auto loan fraud by Tricolor Holdings. Fifth Third Bancorp shareholders are encouraged to contact Lowey Dannenberg for information on potential remedies.

feed.title: Rothschild & Co Redburn Lowers Delta Air Lines, Inc. (DAL) Price Target to $70
feed.summary: Rothschild & Co Redburn has lowered its price target for Delta Air Lines (DAL) to $70 from $72, citing accelerating domestic airline capacity growth and potential disruptive pressures from geopolitical tensions and increased fuel costs. Despite maintaining a Buy rating, the firm warned of substantial downward revisions to industry consensus estimates due to higher fuel prices. Separately, Delta announced significant leadership changes, including new appointments for Chief Operating Officer, President of Delta TechOps, and Chief Financial Officer, following several executive retirements and departures.

feed.title: Morgan Stanley Raises KeyCorp (KEY) Price Target to $26
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley has increased its price target for KeyCorp (KEY) to $26 from $24, maintaining an "Equal Weight" rating, citing optimism for the banking sector due to potential tailwinds like stronger loan growth and expanding net interest margins. This follows an upgrade by Robert W. Baird & Co. to "Neutral" from "Underperform" due to valuation after a selloff in regional bank stocks, arguing that fears of AI disruption and severe credit deterioration are excessive. KeyCorp, a major U.S. regional bank, is seen as having a balanced risk-reward profile, with AI adoption expected to benefit banks through efficiency rather than disruption.

feed.title: Atlas Energy to buy $840M in power assets from Caterpillar
feed.summary: Atlas Energy has inked a multiyear agreement with Caterpillar to acquire 1.4 gigawatts of natural gas power generation assets for $840 million between 2027 and 2029. This strategic move aims to build out private grid infrastructure to meet the surging electrical demand from data centers and manufacturing reshoring efforts. Caterpillar's power systems segment, which includes power generation solutions, has become its fastest-growing segment, driven by high demand for backup and primary power solutions due to public grid constraints.

feed.title: Hundreds of thousands of renters will see refunds. Are you one of them?
feed.summary: The Federal Trade Commission announced a settlement with Invitation Homes, a major landlord, that will result in over 444,000 renters receiving refund checks. The company was accused of charging undisclosed mandatory fees and unfairly withholding deposits, among other deceptive practices. Renters who paid $45 or more in covered fees between January 2021 and September 2024 are eligible, with an average refund of about $106.
